title: AI Trend Signals - Relevance to Jayakrishna's Current Stack
content:
Signal 1 - Applied RAG over static prompting: Jayakrishna prioritizes retrieval-augmented generation using LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, FAISS, and semantic search for document intelligence workflows. Practical implementation is visible in his production RAG pipelines and PDF-to-podcast system.

Signal 2 - Local/private LLM inference for cost and control: He runs local inference with Ollama and models such as LLaMA, Mistral, and Phi. This trend maps to his Batcave architecture where he operates a private AI platform with hybrid-cloud caching and low-latency retrieval goals.

Signal 3 - Evaluation-first GenAI development: He uses evaluation methods like BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, A/B testing, and human-in-the-loop feedback. This indicates a tooling choice toward measurable model quality instead of prompt-only iteration.

Signal 4 - MLOps and observability convergence: His delivery stack combines MLflow/model versioning, CI/CD (GitHub Actions and Jenkins), drift detection, and Netdata/Telegram alerting. This trend signal is relevant because his work repeatedly emphasizes production reliability and monitoring, not only model training.

Signal 5 - CPU-efficient, containerized AI operations: He built and operates a 56-container hybrid-cloud platform running 36+ services across 177GB of production data. This aligns with current industry movement toward practical, cost-aware infrastructure for continuous AI workloads.
